title: The Tiny Story of Ghrelin and Leptin

pages.heading: The Tiny Story of Ghrelin and Leptin
pages.text: Open this little book to meet two body messengers that help us notice hunger and fullness.

pages.heading: Meet the tiny guides
pages.text: Pip, Poppy, and Pebble learn that ghrelin is a messenger that can say, 'Your body may need food soon.' Leptin is a messenger that can help say, 'Your body has energy stored.'

pages.heading: The little problem
pages.text: The guides wonder why hunger does not feel the same every day. They learn that sleep, meals, growth, activity, and routines can all affect hunger and fullness signals.

pages.heading: What we learned
pages.text: Ghrelin and leptin are part of a helpful body conversation. Listening kindly to the body, eating balanced meals, resting, and asking trusted adults or clinicians questions can support that conversation.
